g.data-narration: Welcome to Module 3: Blockchain Wallets. Let's explore how these essential tools secure your digital assets.
A blockchain wallet is a digital tool that manages cryptographic keys. It doesn't store currency—it stores the keys to access your funds on the blockchain.
Every wallet has two essential components: a public key (like your bank account number) and a private key (like your PIN).
Your public key is derived mathematically from your private key. It acts as your receiving address—anyone can send funds to it.
g.text: • Derived from private key using elliptic curve cryptography
• Acts like your bank account number - share freely to receive funds

g.data-narration: The private key is a secret 256-bit number. It's the master key—anyone with it has complete control over your funds. Losing it means losing access forever.
g.text: • A randomly generated 256-bit number (64 hex characters)
• Master key that proves ownership - never reveal to anyone
⚠️ Losing your private key means permanent loss of funds!

g.data-narration: When you send crypto, your wallet uses your private key to create a digital signature. The network verifies this without ever seeing your private key.
g.text: ⚙️ How a Wallet Works: Digital Signatures

g.data-narration: Wallets come in two custody models: custodial (third party holds your keys) and non-custodial (you hold your keys).
Hot wallets are connected to the internet—convenient for frequent transactions but more vulnerable to online attacks.
g.text: 🔥 Hot Wallets (Online) - Convenient but Less Secure

g.data-narration: Cold wallets stay offline, offering maximum security for long-term storage. Hardware wallets sign transactions without exposing keys.
A seed phrase is a 12-24 word backup that can restore your entire wallet. It's your ultimate recovery option—store it safely offline!
g.text: • BIP39 standard: 12, 18, or 24 words from standardized wordlist
• Regenerates ALL private keys and addresses in your wallet
⚠️ NEVER store digitally! Write on paper. Anyone with this phrase owns your funds.

g.data-narration: HD (Hierarchical Deterministic) wallets derive unlimited addresses from a single seed, improving privacy and simplifying backups.
g.text: • BIP32/BIP44 standards: Generate unlimited addresses from ONE seed
• Single backup (seed phrase) restores ALL addresses and keys
• New address for each transaction enhances privacy

g.data-narration: Let's review the key takeaways: wallets manage keys not currency, protect your private keys, choose wallet types based on your needs, and always backup your seed phrase.
g.text: ✓ Public Key = Shareable address | Private Key = Must remain secret
✓ Hot Wallets = Convenient | Cold Wallets = Secure for HODLing
✓ Seed Phrase = Master backup - Store offline, never digitally!

g.data-narration: This knowledge of wallets prepares you for understanding transactions and dApps. Next, we'll explore how transactions are broadcast and verified.
g.text: 🔜 Up Next: Transaction Lifecycle & Network Verification
Understanding how transactions propagate through the network
